original text:
Premiums for homebuyers with a down payment of 10% or more and for CMHC's portfolio insurance and multi-unit insurance products remain unchanged. The changes do not apply to mortgages currently insured by CMHC.
CMHC is mandated to operate its mortgage loan insurance business on a commercial basis. The premiums and fees it collects and the investment income it earns cover related claims and other expenses while providing a reasonable rate of return on its capital holding target.
CMHC contributes to the stability of Canada's housing finance system, including housing markets, by providing qualified Canadians in all parts of the country with access to a range of housing finance options in both good and bad economic times.
CMHC reviews its premiums on an annual basis and will announce decisions on premiums following this review.
Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation (CMHC) has been Canada's authority on housing for more than 65 years.
CMHC's new premium rates will be effective for new mortgage loan insurance requests submitted on or after June 1, 2015. The current mortgage loan insurance premiums will apply for applications submitted to CMHC prior to June 1, 2015, regardless of the closing date. As is normal practice, complete borrower and property details must be submitted to CMHC when requesting mortgage loan insurance.
The increase applies to mortgage loan insurance premiums for residential housing of 1 and 2 units for homebuyers with less than a 10% down payment.
CMHC's capital holdings reduce Canadian taxpayers' exposure to the housing market and contribute to the long term stability of the financial system. In August 2014, CMHC increased its capital holding target from 200% to 220% of the minimum OSFI requirements.
In 2014, the average CMHC insured loan at 95% loan-to-value was $252,530. Based on this figure, the higher premium will result in an increase of approximately $5 to the monthly mortgage payment for the average Canadian homebuyer. This is not expected to have a material impact on housing markets.
Premiums in Manitoba, Ontario and Quebec are subject to provincial sales tax  -  the sales tax cannot be added to the loan amount.

Premiums in Manitoba, Ontario and Quebec are subject to provincial sales tax  -  the sales tax cannot be added to the loan amount.

original text:
Birds Of A Feather: Anniversary Giveaway!
It's been a year since we started our blog, see here. Wow, time flies when your having fun. We thought we'd celebrate with our first ever give away. Traditionally, paper is the first anniversary gift. I don't know about you, but what a crappy gift. So in honor of our anniversary, we won't give you paper and are thrilled that Shabby Apple is partnering with us to make our anniversary special. Shabby Apple has amazing dresses in vintage styles at great prices.
This dress is very "Hooper"
And would wear this all summer long.
But picked this one to kick it into Fall. Can you believe that we both chose this one as our top pick for the give away. Yes, we agreed on something -- it must be a great dress.
You can pair it with whatever you want when it's yours.
PS: Would you like me to post about your giveaway on my blog to direct people to your great giveaway and blog?
fun dresses. loving the boots with them. and i can't wait to wear boots with these "anthro" type dresses! i have a pair of boots kindof like your fave. i got them for 4 bucks at the goodwill!
all i need is a free dress and i'm good to go for the Fall!
hee hee. oh...and i already follow!
love the dresses as much as i enjoy your blog. happy anniversary!
Happy 1st Blog Birthday! I've been following you on my google reader. Sometimes my student find me laughing out loud because I'm reading your blog instead of grading their papers! Love the dresses :) Green is one of my colors.
That dress would look so cute with my fringed moccasin boots!! So pick me!!
And I am now a follower, should have done it sooner. And Happy Birthday!!
I love giveaways ;) can a preggo fit in that dress????
Yall's blog always makes me laugh, love it!
Love your blog and the dress!
This is such a great giveaway. Congrats on your anniversary!
Congrats on you anniversary! Please pick me that dress is awesome!
Congrats - and thanks for the chance. I follow.
Beautiful dress. Thanks for the giveaway! Happy Anniversary!

original text:
When I started this company in 2002 I struggled as anyone who starts a new enterprise with limited funds does.
to create a marketable product, deducted profit margins, making it very hard to grow the business.
I understand that a business must make a profit but I also experienced a lot of greed.
I held on and grew the company slowly employing the three aspects which ultimately lead to success: price, quality and service.
your life just a little bit easier!
Due to the US exchange rate along with increases in shipping and brokerage I had to adjust my prices accordingly.
Lip balm tube and tin prices include shipping (expedited) and handling for Canada.
Overseas shipping is expensive (expedited-air). Ocean is much cheaper but takes 4-6 weeks.
Please let us know if you prefer this and we will gladly send you a quote.
Stock - white, black, clear.
Tubes and caps are sold separately. I did this because previously (when they were sold as a unit) many customers did not want only white caps but wanted a variety.
The price (as separate units) is still the same as it was when I sold them as a complete unit and with shipping included I do believe it is the best price available.
Sleek, smooth top, - 2 oz tins .... Classy!
Lithographed tins. Great prices - 15,000 minimum.
Sample Package - 28.00 All sizes and styles of tubes and tins including printed tins.

original text:
The Fourth " But he had a welcome mat!
But he had a welcome mat!
Do you want to know how dedicated to this comic I am?! I drew most of this in a car, on my vacation (to the snowy mountains, in fact) - that's how much! It wasn't easy, either. It caused for my hand to jump around a lot, leading to some weird expression quirks that I think actually turned out for the better.
I learned a lot from this comic. Not just how to draw in the car- I realized I had no clue what a syrup bottle looks like off the top of my head.
ik...in order to do it, i need to buy a tablet. u kno, those digital art thingys.
Hehe! I know very well, silly. I use one to make this comic and most other things I draw. They are SO WORTH IT. And I've had mine for five or six years; it still works great.
Hey now, we wouldn't want to milk stereotypes for humor, now would we?
I've noticed a lot in comics and other things that people who slam open doors usually know what's on the other side of it... Case in point, the lumberjack slammed open the door and immediately started yelling, so he must have already known that villains were in his cottage, drinking his syrup. Also, I spy with my little eye two voltorbs in the crowd!
Gotta say though, I think I laughed more for this page than any other.
He saw them through that massive window which seems to have been open during a snow flurry!
We are totally above making fun of Canadians.
The NPCs from Idenau's northernmost provinces, however, are fair game.
This page is great because I just got back from a vacation to Niagra Falls.
Is that so? I hope you had fun! I went there a few years back. It was the only time I've been to Canada and I actually did hear a lot of people saying "eh?" I was highly amused.
I sure did! My family and I stayed at a really nice hotel called Embassy Suites, where we enjoyed a complementary buffet breakfast and a room on the 21st floor with an amazing view of the falls. Sure enough, the first guy we spoke to had that funny Wisconsin accent, eh? But we were much more amused by the other tourist groups and some of the people at our hotel: Amish, Asians, Hasidic Jews, Indians, people in turbans... the list goes on.
Yeah, my family stays at Embassy Suites often on our vacations. My parents enjoy the free drinks in the afternoon. =P And I will too next year when I'm 21.
dude....is that the wizard from the temple of generic er doom? at the head of the mob?
No, it's the lumberjack, silly Although they both have SUCH INTENSE EYEBROWS that they must be related.
...I repeat my comment from the last page, and use this one as proof about the lumberjacks.

original text:
The pupils in Years 3 and 4 have worked extremely hard this term to produce a superb musical version of Treasure Island.
They performed with great enthusiasm and singing and acting skills were excellent.
They received many well-deserved compliments from the audience and clearly felt very proud of their achievements.
Adam: I liked it when it was time for an encore - everybody was joining in.
Hector: That was the best play I've ever done.
Joshua: This is the best play I've ever been in and it was extremely fun.
Ivo: My part in the play may not be very big but it was fun bringing on the treasure chest and sniggering at one of the jokes.
Atholl: My favourite line was "Avast, you scurvy rodent", because everyone laughed.
Ben: My favourite song was ‘Cheese on toast' because it made the audience laugh.
Tiegan: When the play started I was really scared but when we got on the stage it was calming.
Flora: I thought the play was amazing and my favourite bit was when Ben Gun caught a fish.
Thomas: My favourite part of the play was when Samara (a mouse) tried to climb up Jim's arm!
Amber: The play was so exciting! It is a very funny play because Jack says, "I've had enough of ships I'd be better off on the wagon". It made a lot of parents laugh.
